simulator: fresh-context general-purpose agent (sonnet), zero audit context, 2026-07-11

tasks.friction: First instinct (golden-path.sh, named in CLAUDE.md's Golden Path table) is actually a sourced function library, not an executable — but its exit-2 error self-documented the fix, naming the two real machine-readable scripts verbatim. Needed 2 scripts since no single call returns all three of state+health+next-command.
Reference docs partially duplicate each other; --json coverage split across two files; loa-capabilities.sh not discovered.
None beyond the deliberate typo — 'Did you mean: --json?' made recovery mechanical, not investigative.
None — usage + option table + status-ladder/exit-code legend.

overall_impression: Felt like a mature, agent-aware CLI surface: scripts consistently support --json/--help, and failure modes are self-teaching (did-you-mean suggestions, wrong-script-use-X-instead messages) rather than opaque stack traces. Snags: golden-path naming mismatch vs the machine-readable entry points; mildly duplicated discovery docs.
observed_validations: R-013 guard message drove successful recovery (T1)
residual_friction_closed_in_pass1: golden-path guard now names loa-status --triage (one call for T1)
